section: P1.7 Batch And Cache Vocabulary Work
verbatim: Gather all plan embedding texts before vocabulary/tree/funnel fan-out.
action: Build gather-before-fanout in the retriever: collect every plan lane text needing an embedding (planner lanes, vocabulary resolution queries, summary_tree descent queries, Funnel A breadth) into one embed batch before any Qdrant fan-out; verify with a trace probe asserting exactly one embedder call batch per query where lanes share texts.
where: backend/services/retriever/ (query_plan.py fan-out seam, vocabulary.py, tree descent, funnel path); embedder_mlx :8082 client
why: One embed round-trip instead of N serial sidecar calls directly cuts vocabulary/tree p50 on the shared Metal GPU.
feasibility_note: Unslotted in the S0-S14 table but named a P2.2 prerequisite via P1.7 (PLAN_CRITIQUE S10 row: 'P1.7 batching+lexical-only baseline (S0/S5)'), so it must close in the S5 window before S10. Terminology: 'vocabulary' here = the query-time vocabulary RESOLUTION stage, not the corpus_lexicon store (registry_alias 'vocabulary' OVERLAPPING — call the store corpus_lexicon); 'tree' = summary_tree (registry_hierarchy canonical); 'funnel' = Funnel A parent-level summary breadth, not Tier-0 doc_summaries (registry_hierarchy 'doc summary' collision).
required_receipt: Retrieval trace on a multi-lane query showing a single batched embed call covering plan+vocabulary+tree texts (embed_calls counter before/after), plus vocabulary p50 delta vs the pre-change latency probe; asserting test exit 0.

section: P1.7 Batch And Cache Vocabulary Work
verbatim: Reuse vectors across planner, vocabulary, tree, and retrieval lanes.
action: Implement per-request vector reuse: key embedded texts by normalized text within one retrieval request and pass the same vector object to planner, vocabulary resolution, summary_tree descent, and recall-lane search instead of re-embedding; assert via a counter test that identical lane texts embed exactly once per request.
where: backend/services/retriever/ (shared embed cache per request context); embedder_mlx :8082 call sites
why: Duplicate embeddings of the same query text are pure wasted GPU time on the contended Metal device.
feasibility_note: Companion to L701; same S5 window and same P2.2-prereq pressure (PLAN_CRITIQUE S10). Registry note applies: bare 'planner' is ambiguous — deterministic QueryPlanV2 (retriever/query_plan.py) vs LLM grounded_planner are different components (registry_retrieval 'grounded planner'); this item concerns the deterministic planner's lane texts. Scope reuse per-request only; cross-request reuse is L706's cache and must not be duplicated here (Standing Rule 2 discipline).
required_receipt: Asserting test: one retrieval request with N lanes sharing a text embeds it once (embed-call counter == unique texts); probe exit 0 in the deployed backend container.

section: P1.7 Batch And Cache Vocabulary Work
verbatim: Batch Qdrant dense lane requests per corpus where client/server contracts support it.
action: Convert per-lane serial Qdrant dense searches into query_batch_points batches grouped by target collection/corpus (client already gRPC per ledger L753); land behind a flag, then A/B vocabulary+funnel stage wall time before/after.
where: backend/services/retriever/vocabulary.py dense lane calls; corpus_{cid8}_* collections; summary-tree client (already batched per L753)
why: Batching removes per-request round-trip overhead on the hot path, the cheapest remaining Qdrant latency win.
feasibility_note: Ledger's own baseline (L753) confirms the contract works: summary-tree searches already use query_batch_points, so 'where client/server contracts support it' is proven for the same server (Qdrant 1.18.0). NOTE this item is stated TWICE in the ledger — here and P1.9 L761 are the same work; execute once, stamp both (critique C4 ledger-hygiene class). Must respect L705's separation rule.
required_receipt: Trace showing vocabulary dense lookups issued as query_batch_points grouped per collection; per-stage Qdrant call count drops with unchanged result sets (diff of returned point IDs pre/post = empty); probe exit 0.

section: P1.7 Batch And Cache Vocabulary Work
verbatim: Keep exact alias payload lookup logically separate from dense requests.
action: While implementing L703/L761 batching, keep the exact alias payload lookup (scroll/filter on aliases_normalized in the corpus_{cid8}_schemas lexicon points) as a separate call path from approximate dense query_batch_points; add an asserting test that exact-alias results are byte-identical pre/post batching.
who: junior-agent (design constraint enforced in the L703 change)
where: backend/services/retriever/vocabulary.py (_TRUSTED_EXACT_ALIAS_METHODS path); Qdrant corpus_{cid8}_schemas payload aliases_normalized
why: Exact alias hits are deterministic identity evidence; folding them into ANN search would make exact matches probabilistic and silently lossy.
feasibility_note: This is a correctness constraint on L703, not independent work — schedule as one change. Terminology: 'alias' is 5-way overloaded (registry_alias 'aliases' OVERLAPPING); here it means the lexicon alias (identity evidence) payload fields (aliases/aliases_normalized on schemas points), the only retrieval-live meaning — not curated CONCEPT_ALIASES, latent aliases, or graph alias maps.
required_receipt: Asserting test in the batching PR: exact-alias resolution results identical before/after batching (set diff empty), and code path shows separate scroll vs query_batch_points calls; exit 0.

section: P1.7 Batch And Cache Vocabulary Work
verbatim: Cache vocabulary resolution by query hash, selected corpus set, planner version, and artifact epoch. [IN CODE — services/retriever/vocabulary_cache.py + resolve() wiring: key = normalized query + ordered lane queries + sorted corpus set + tier/top-k/disabled/exclusions + per-corpus epoch; TTL default 300s, LRU 512, env-tunable; awaiting live verify]
receipt: IN CODE: services/retriever/vocabulary_cache.py, TTL 300s, LRU 512, awaiting live verify

where: backend/services/retriever/vocabulary_cache.py + resolve() wiring in vocabulary.py; deployed backend container

Checklist @336dc65 shows [x] with the LIVE-VERIFIED 2026-07-13 receipt (commit 327da45), exactly the S0 deliverable in PLAN_CRITIQUE row S0 ('live-verify P1.7 vocabulary cache'). File exists on main (backend/services/retriever/vocabulary_cache.py). Cross-process TTL bound (300s staleness window for worker writes) is a documented contract limitation, acceptable per module contract.

section: P1.7 Batch And Cache Vocabulary Work
verbatim: Invalidate cache on lexicon/tree/document artifact changes. [IN CODE — epoch bumps from lexicon materialization (full + affected) and corpus-lexicon deletion; cross-process worker writes are bounded by the TTL, stated explicitly in the module contract]
receipt: IN CODE: epoch bumps on lexicon materialization + corpus-lexicon deletion; cross-process bounded by TTL
action: Accept receipt commit 327da45 (S0, verified 2026-07-13): bump_corpus_epoch rotated the cache key (070c7428… -> 3c35e72b…), invalidations=1, next identical resolve() recomputed (miss, 0.21s) instead of serving stale. Re-exercise for real at S5: the lexicon rematerialization epoch bump is the designed live test (PLAN_CRITIQUE S5 row).
who: junior-agent (receipt acceptance); S5 rematerialization re-exercises it
where: backend/services/retriever/vocabulary_cache.py epoch keys; lexicon materialization + corpus-lexicon deletion call sites
why: Without epoch invalidation the cache would serve pre-rebuild vocabulary after every lexicon/tree change — a silent staleness bug.
feasibility_note: Claim row stale ('awaiting live verify'); ledger @336dc65 shows [x] LIVE-VERIFIED 2026-07-13 (commit 327da45). Known bounded gap stated in the module contract: cross-process worker writes are only TTL-bounded (300s) — an ingest-worker-container write (offline-ingest overlay runs ingest in a separate container) will not bump the backend process's epoch until TTL expiry; acceptable now, re-check at S5 when rematerialization runs from the worker path.
required_receipt: Commit 327da45 receipt (key rotation + invalidations=1 + forced recompute, exit 0); S5 re-receipt: after lexicon rematerialization, first resolve() is a miss with new epoch in key.

section: P1.7 Batch And Cache Vocabulary Work
verbatim: Capture a lexical/exact-only resolver baseline before adding deterministic multi-point representations so each representation method must prove lift.
action: Before any P2.2 representation points exist, run and persist a lexical/exact-only resolver baseline: execute vocabulary resolution with dense lanes disabled (exact alias + lexical match only) across the held-out query set, recording per-query resolved concepts + latency to a docs/baselines artifact; every later representation method must beat it.
who: script (new probe under backend/scripts/), junior-agent runs it
where: backend/services/retriever/vocabulary.py (exact/lexical path); docs/baselines/ artifact; held-out eval set (P1.1)
why: Without a lexical-only floor, multi-point vectors can claim lift they do not have — the 'prove lift' discipline that gates P2.2.
feasibility_note: Explicit S10 prerequisite (PLAN_CRITIQUE S10: 'P1.7 batching+lexical-only baseline (S0/S5)'). Terminology: 'baseline' is 3-way overloaded (registry_eval 'baseline' OVERLAPPING — census/latency/eval species); this one is an EVAL-baseline artifact and must be filed under docs/baselines with its own name, not conflated with BASELINE_2026-07-13.json (census) or probe_tier_latency outputs (latency). Run it AFTER S5's registry/lexicon rematerialization so it baselines the artifact P2.2 will actually compete against.
required_receipt: docs/baselines/LEXICAL_ONLY_RESOLVER_<date>.json with per-query resolution+latency over the held-out set, referenced from the ledger line; command + exit 0 recorded.

This is a standing release gate, matching P2.2's checklist prereq block ('4 prereqs incl. P1.7', PLAN_CRITIQUE sec.1 P2.2 row). '18-second resolver' traces to the pre-cache vocabulary stage pathology; post-S0 cache (L706) reduces repeats but the cold path is what the gate protects.

Recall check must diff resolved concepts, not answer text.

section: P1.7 Batch And Cache Vocabulary Work

where: deployed backend container /api/chat; vocabulary_cache stats endpoint/trace metadata

'Safely' = identical resolution + correct invalidation, both already demonstrated at the unit seam, so residual risk is wiring-level (trace metadata plumbing). Live probe recipe exists (memory: mint JWT via auth_service.create_access_token; diagnostics ride the 'Local RAG retrieval' DONE trace).

section: P1.8 Finish Sidecar Isolation
verbatim: [x] Reranker performs real inference warmup at startup.
action: Re-verify reranker startup warmup on the DEPLOYED sidecar: restart the :8081 service (launchctl kickstart), then check its health/readiness for warmup-complete status and confirm the first rerank call after restart shows no compile-cost spike (compare first-call vs steady-state latency).
where: :8081 reranker sidecar (torch fp16 cross-encoder, plist-managed host service); rollback backend reranker_mlx
why: An unwarmed reranker makes the first user query pay multi-second model compile cost.
feasibility_note: Ledger [x] but carries NO receipt (unlike the three embedder boxes S0 stamped). Registry warning (registry_leftover 'reranker readiness'): the DEPLOYED :8081 binary is the torch fp16 cross-encoder while reranker_mlx is the rollback — verify which binary serves :8081 before trusting warmup semantics, since warmup may have been verified on the other backend. Also: sidecars deploy from a COPY in ~/PolymathRuntime (memory), so repo code having warmup does not prove the running copy does. Standing Rule 3: restamp with date once probed.
required_receipt: Post-restart :8081 health showing warmup/ready status + first-rerank-call latency within ~1.5x steady-state; ledger line restamped '(re-verified <date>, torch_fp16 backend)'.

why: Admission classes are the mechanism that keeps backfill from starving interactive query embedding on the shared Metal GPU.

Registry duplicate-implementation warning (registry_leftover 'embedder /health'): TWO embedder services exist — repo /embedder docker (NO warmup/admission code) vs deployed embedder_mlx host sidecar :8082; all verification must target :8082, and any probe touching the repo /embedder is verifying the wrong binary.

section: P1.8 Finish Sidecar Isolation

Distinct from acceptance L786 (ingestion/backfill callers use LOWER classes) and L796 (p95 protection under backfill); verifying this box is the cheap third of that triangle. Note the S0 warmup receipt itself says the warmup batch ran 'at backfill class' through the serving-path gate, proving class plumbing works end-to-end for at least one class.
required_receipt: Grep hits showing interactive class on query-path embed calls + one contention probe trace (query admitted while backfill batch queued); ledger restamp with date.

section: P1.9 Qdrant Hot-Path And Contention Audit
verbatim: Warm dense child search at k=85: about 6 ms p50 without payload and about 14 ms p50 with the full payload; 30-run p95 stayed below 20 ms.
action: Re-run the warm dense child search probe at k=85 on the same largest corpus (30 runs, with and without payload) and compare p50/p95 to ~6ms/~14ms/<20ms; this is the 'measured target' that acceptance L782 references.
where: Qdrant corpus_{cid8} child collection of the largest active corpus; gRPC client path
why: These numbers prove Qdrant itself is NOT the latency problem (ms-scale), pinning blame for slow queries on upstream stages.
feasibility_note: Fresh (2026-07-12) measured fact with a clear method (30-run p50/p95, k=85). The 6ms-vs-14ms payload delta is the direct motivation for L758's field selectors; the <20ms p95 gives L782 its numeric target. No contradiction with plan-critique receipts. Re-measurement is cheap and should be re-captured whenever the collection is rewritten (S5/S7 payload rewrite).
required_receipt: Probe output: 30-run p50/p95 at k=85 with/without payload on the same collection, filed with the run date beside the ledger baseline.

section: P1.9 Qdrant Hot-Path And Contention Audit
verbatim: Full payload transfer was about 292 KB for 85 hits; a bounded payload selector reduced it to about 148 KB.
action: Re-check by measuring response byte size for one k=85 full-payload search (~292 KB expected) and the same search under the bounded selector (~148 KB); keep the selector's field list as the draft for L758.
where: Qdrant largest-corpus child collection; retriever payload selector draft
why: Quantifies the transfer saving (~50%) that L758's field selectors will bank on the hot path.
feasibility_note: Measured 2026-07-12 with an actual bounded-selector trial, so L758 is de-risked: the selector mechanism demonstrably works against this Qdrant version and roughly halves bytes. The open question L758 must answer is field COMPLETENESS (ranking/hydration/citations/diagnostics consumers), not transfer mechanics.
required_receipt: Byte counts for the same query under with_payload=True vs the explicit selector, plus the selector field list used.

section: P1.9 Qdrant Hot-Path And Contention Audit
verbatim: Qdrant used 6.63/8 GiB (82.9%) with no optimizer job active.
action: Re-check current Qdrant memory (docker stats / container memory endpoint) against the 6.63/8 GiB (82.9%) reading and against whatever threshold the readiness memory warning gate uses; record headroom before any index-adding work (L763) or bulk pass (S4).
where: Qdrant container (8 GiB limit); readiness memory gate config
why: At 83% with no optimizer running, memory is the binding constraint every P1.9 change must not worsen.
feasibility_note: Verified-dated fact, but the most perishable number in the section: 82.9% is already close to any sane warning gate, which makes acceptance L780 tight TODAY, makes L763 (adding a summary_model index) a memory SPEND, and makes L765 (removing unused indexes) and the 37 empty collections (L751) the funding source. Sequencing implication: do L765 cleanup before or with L763 additions.
required_receipt: Fresh memory reading with date + the configured warning-gate threshold, filed with the P1.9 baseline.

section: P1.9 Qdrant Hot-Path And Contention Audit
verbatim: The node held 59 collections / about 2.67M vector representations, including 37 empty collections and non-active nonempty collections.
action: Re-verify the census: list collections with point/vector counts; confirm ~59 collections / ~2.67M vector representations / 37 empty; classify each nonempty non-active collection (owner corpus, purpose, last-write) as input to cleanup and P3.3 consolidation.
where: Qdrant collections API; cross-reference Mongo corpora registry
why: Empty and orphaned collections carry index RAM overhead on a node already at 83% memory.
feasibility_note: Verified-dated fact. The 37 empty collections are the cheapest memory recovery available, but deletion is a destructive op — per P0.6 discipline it needs the deletion/orphan machinery (lease + reconciliation) and an owner-visible list, not an ad-hoc drop; and permanent deletion decisions belong to the owner. P3.3 (collection consolidation, S13) is the structural fix; do not front-run it with risky merges — only provably-orphaned EMPTY collections are pre-S13 candidates.
required_receipt: Census table (collection, points, vectors, corpus owner, empty y/n) + for any deletion: owner-approved list and post-delete memory delta.

section: P1.9 Qdrant Hot-Path And Contention Audit
verbatim: Hot search clients already use internal gRPC. Summary-tree searches already use query_batch_points; broad vocabulary/funnel batching remains incomplete.
action: Re-check by grep: confirm hot search clients construct gRPC Qdrant clients (prefer_grpc/internal gRPC) and that summary-tree search uses query_batch_points, while vocabulary/funnel call sites still issue unbatched per-lane searches — the gap L703/L761 closes.

why: Establishes that transport is already optimal and batching is the only remaining client-side gap.
feasibility_note: Consistent with the rest of the section and with L703's feasibility (the batched contract is proven in the tree path). This fact prevents wasted work: no gRPC migration is needed, only call-shape changes. Keep the claim scoped — 'hot' clients; batch/ingest writers may differ and are governed by L768 instead.
required_receipt: Grep evidence: gRPC client flags at retriever client construction + query_batch_points in tree search + absence in vocabulary/funnel paths (or their new presence once L703 lands).

section: P1.9 Qdrant Hot-Path And Contention Audit
verbatim: Add per-stage Qdrant wall/server timing, result bytes, collection, filter shape, and candidate count to retrieval traces.
action: Add per-stage Qdrant instrumentation to retrieval traces: wall time, server-reported time, result bytes, collection name, filter shape (field names only), candidate count for every Qdrant call; verify by one live query trace containing all six fields per stage.
where: backend retriever Qdrant client wrapper; trace/diagnostics schema (rides the 'Local RAG retrieval' trace metadata)
why: Without stage attribution, every future latency claim (S8 A/B, L782, L784) is a guess about which stage moved.
feasibility_note: Unslotted in S0-S14; it is pure additive instrumentation (no behavior change) and is the enabling dependency for acceptance L784 and the L782/L796 measurements — land it in the S5 window BEFORE the S8 A/B or the A/B loses attribution power. Registry note: this is a finer-grained instrument than the existing three-tier latency probe (probe_tier_latency.py) and complements, not replaces, it (registry_leftover 'latency probes').
required_receipt: One live retrieval trace showing per-Qdrant-call {wall_ms, server_ms, bytes, collection, filter_shape, candidates} for vocabulary/tree/child stages; asserting test exit 0.

section: P1.9 Qdrant Hot-Path And Contention Audit
verbatim: Replace with_payload=True on hot candidate searches with explicit field selectors, preserving every field consumed by ranking, hydration, citations, and diagnostics.
action: Inventory every consumer of hot-path candidate payloads (ranking features, hydration keys, citation fields, diagnostics) into an explicit field list; replace with_payload=True with that selector; assert zero field-miss via a differential test (full-payload run vs selector run must produce identical ranked+cited output).
who: junior-agent (consumer audit + retriever change + differential test)
where: backend retriever hot candidate search calls; ranking/hydration/citation consumers; Qdrant child collections
why: Halves hot-path transfer (292->148 KB per L748) and p50 payload cost (14->6ms band) without touching recall.
feasibility_note: Mechanically proven (L748 measured the selector working), but the risk is the completeness audit: a missed diagnostics or citation field fails SILENTLY (evidence renders without provenance) — the exact silent-fallback class the standing discipline forbids. The differential test (identical ranked output + identical citation fields, full vs selector) is non-negotiable, and the field list must be checked again after S5/S7 payload rewrites add or remove fields (one-migration-per-seam: co-schedule with the S5/S7 payload rewrite so the audit runs once against the final payload shape).
required_receipt: Differential test: same query, with_payload=True vs selector — identical ranked IDs, citations, and diagnostics fields; plus measured bytes drop; exit 0.

section: P1.9 Qdrant Hot-Path And Contention Audit
verbatim: Batch compatible dense vocabulary and tree requests by collection/corpus; do not merge exact alias lookup into the approximate vector request.
action: Duplicate of P1.7 L703+L705 (same work, second ledger location): implement collection/corpus-grouped query_batch_points for dense vocabulary+tree lanes with exact alias lookup kept separate; on completion stamp BOTH ledger lines with the same receipt.
where: backend/services/retriever/vocabulary.py + tree clients; corpus_{cid8}_* collections
why: One receipt closing two ledger lines keeps the checklist's checkbox contract honest (critique C4 hygiene).
feasibility_note: Verbatim restatement of L703 (batching) + L705 (separation) inside the P1.9 section — the ledger states this work twice, and the P1.9 section id itself is duplicated (critique C4: two sections named 'P1.9'). Execute once under L703/L705, cross-stamp here. No independent feasibility question beyond L703's.
required_receipt: Same receipt as L703/L705 (batched-call trace + identical-results diff), referenced from both checklist lines.

section: P1.9 Qdrant Hot-Path And Contention Audit
verbatim: Audit all active query filters against payload indexes before enabling strict mode. summary_model is currently filtered but not indexed.
receipt: finding: summary_model filtered but not indexed
action: Enumerate every active query filter field per collection kind from retriever/graph/readiness/deletion code; diff against existing payload indexes (collection info API); create the missing summary_model index (and any other gaps) via a guarded script; only then may strict mode be considered.
where: Qdrant payload indexes on route collections; retriever filter construction sites; qdrant_writer.py index definitions
why: Filtering on unindexed fields full-scans segments — and under strict mode it would error outright.
feasibility_note: The finding is pre-verified in the ledger ('summary_model is currently filtered but not indexed'). Two cautions: (a) each new index costs RAM on a node at 82.9% (L750) — pair with L765 removals in one pass; (b) index creation on 59 collections is a write burst — run it in the S5 rematerialization window, not during interactive hours (Standing Rule 4: one migration per seam). Strict mode itself is NOT approved by this item; it only removes a blocker.
required_receipt: Audit table (filter field x collection x indexed y/n) with zero unindexed active filters after the pass; Qdrant collection info showing the summary_model index; memory delta recorded against L750.

section: P1.9 Qdrant Hot-Path And Contention Audit
verbatim: Audit the 29 payload indexes repeated on each route collection; retain hot filter fields in RAM and remove only indexes proven unused by retrieval, graph, readiness, deletion, and repair paths.
action: For each of the 29 per-collection payload indexes, produce a proof-of-use table across ALL five consumer path families (retrieval, graph, readiness, deletion, repair) by grepping filter construction; propose removals only for indexes with zero hits in all five; owner reviews the removal list; execute removals in the same S5/S7 payload pass and record memory reclaimed.
where: Qdrant payload indexes on every route collection; backend filter-construction sites across retriever/graph/readiness/deletion/repair
why: Index RAM is the recoverable headroom on an 83%-full node, but a wrongly-dropped index turns a repair path into a full scan.
feasibility_note: The audit is cheap; the removal is the risk — deletion and repair paths run rarely, so 'proven unused by retrieval' is NOT proof of unused (the item's own five-path list is the right bar). The failure mode is silent slowness in an emergency path, discovered during an incident. Hence owner sign-off on the removal list and Standing Rule 4 co-scheduling with the S5/S7 payload rewrite so collections are touched once. Keep 'retain hot filter fields in RAM' as the default; removal is the exception.
required_receipt: Proof-of-use matrix (29 indexes x 5 path families with grep evidence), owner-approved removal list, and post-removal memory delta + one exercised deletion/repair operation timed within norms.

section: P1.9 Qdrant Hot-Path And Contention Audit
verbatim: Define interactive and bulk-ingest Qdrant profiles. Under concurrent writes, benchmark smaller upsert batches and max_optimization_threads=1 before changing defaults.
action: Define two named Qdrant client/config profiles (interactive vs bulk-ingest); then run a concurrent-write benchmark on a clone/canary collection comparing current defaults vs smaller upsert batches and max_optimization_threads=1, measuring query p95 during writes; adopt changes only if the benchmark wins.
who: script (benchmark harness) + junior-agent; owner only if defaults change system-wide
where: Qdrant client config (upsert batch size), collection optimizer config; ingest-worker write path (polymath_v33-ingest-worker-1)
why: The S4 paid backfill is the first sustained concurrent-write load; unbenchmarked defaults risk interactive p95 blowouts during it.
feasibility_note: The item is correctly benchmark-gated ('before changing defaults') — matching the hypothesis-promotion rule (numeric targets set in advance). Schedule the benchmark BEFORE S4's pair backfill so the bulk profile exists when the heavy write pass runs; UGO_CORPUS canary is the designated guinea pig. Note writes originate from the ingest-worker container, not the backend (offline-ingest overlay) — profile plumbing must reach that container's client.
required_receipt: Benchmark table: query p50/p95 during concurrent writes under {default, small-batch, small-batch+opt_threads=1}; adopted profile named in config with the winning numbers; interactive p95 during S4 within the L746-derived target.

section: P1.9 Qdrant Hot-Path And Contention Audit
verbatim: Do not enable prevent_unoptimized until every write explicitly uses the intended wait semantics and readiness proves deferred points are searchable.
action: Keep prevent_unoptimized OFF (standing gate). Before any future enable: audit every upsert/delete call for explicit wait semantics and add a readiness check proving deferred (unoptimized-segment) points are returned by search; only then bring an enable proposal to the owner.
who: owner (any future enable decision); junior-agent (precondition audit if proposed)
where: Qdrant collection config; every writer call site (qdrant_writer.py, ingest-worker, backfill scripts) wait= parameters; readiness probes
why: Enabling it prematurely turns ingest-time optimizer lag into user-visible write failures or invisible unsearchable points.
feasibility_note: Correctly framed as a do-not-until invariant; nothing in S0-S14 requires prevent_unoptimized, so the default disposition is 'never, unless a concrete failure motivates it'. The precondition (explicit wait semantics on every write) is auditable by grep, but the second precondition (readiness proves deferred points searchable) has no current instrument — it would need a new probe. Treat as blocked-by-design until an owner-level need appears.
required_receipt: Standing: config dump showing prevent_unoptimized disabled. If ever proposed: wait-semantics audit table + deferred-point searchability probe green, plus owner approval note.

section: P1.9 Qdrant Hot-Path And Contention Audit
verbatim: Keep current single-shard/single-replica topology on this one-node Mac; replicas and extra shards are not a free local speedup.
action: Keep single-shard/single-replica on the one-node Mac (standing gate): assert in the collection-creation code/config that shard_number=1, replication_factor=1, and add a census check to the readiness/census probes flagging any collection that deviates.
who: backend service (collection creation defaults) + census probe
where: qdrant_writer.py collection creation params; census/readiness probes
why: Extra shards/replicas on one node add merge and memory overhead with zero availability gain.
feasibility_note: Physics-sound invariant for a single-node deployment (RAM at 82.9% makes replicas actively harmful). No plan item proposes sharding; P3.3 consolidation (S13) is the only structural change on the horizon and it reduces, not increases, collection count. Cheap to enforce mechanically in the census probe rather than as tribal knowledge.
required_receipt: Census probe output: all collections shard_number=1, replication_factor=1; creation-path default asserted by test.

section: P1.9 Qdrant Hot-Path And Contention Audit
verbatim: Pin the Qdrant image version after the performance contract is validated; do not deploy latest as the production data-store contract.
action: After the P1.9 performance targets are validated (L780-L784 green), pin the exact Qdrant image digest (qdrant:1.18.0@sha256:...) in docker-compose and record the pin + validation date in the ledger; forbid 'latest' in any compose file via a CI/grep check.
who: junior-agent (compose pin + grep guard); owner informed of the pinned version
where: docker-compose.yml (+ override/overlay files — all three compose files per deploy topology); ledger baseline header
why: An unpinned data-store image can silently change performance and on-disk format under the validated contract.
feasibility_note: Trivially feasible; the only judgment is timing — the item itself says pin AFTER the performance contract is validated, which lands at the S14 regression/deploy gate (baselines re-captured there). Interim risk: L744's baseline is against 1.18.0 while the tag may float; if any unexplained perf shift appears before S14, pin immediately at 1.18.0 rather than waiting. Deploy gotcha from memory: recreation must merge ALL compose files (override drops kill sidecars silently) — the pin edit must go in the file actually used by the deploy command.
required_receipt: Compose diff showing digest-pinned Qdrant image + grep proving no ':latest' remains + a restart proving the pinned image serves (telemetry version matches pin).

section: P1.9 Qdrant Hot-Path And Contention Audit
verbatim: Qdrant remains below its memory warning gate during normal retrieval.
action: Wire/verify a memory gate check: readiness or monitoring asserts Qdrant memory below its warning threshold during a standard retrieval load probe; run it after every P1.9 change and during S4/S11 bulk passes.
who: script (resource probe in latency harness); backend readiness gate
where: Qdrant container memory stats; readiness memory warning gate config
why: At 82.9% baseline, any regression (new indexes, more collections) tips into swap/OOM territory where all latency numbers die.
feasibility_note: Acceptance framed as a continuous condition, so treat as a standing gate, not a one-shot box. It is TIGHT today: 6.63/8 GiB (L750) leaves <1.4 GiB headroom, and L763 adds indexes while S4/S5 add points — the box likely only stays green if L765/L751 reclamation lands in the same window. Define the numeric warning threshold explicitly (it must exist as config, not vibes) before calling this checked.
required_receipt: Probe output during normal retrieval + during a bulk pass showing memory below the configured warning threshold, with the threshold value named; attached to each P1.9 change receipt.

section: P1.9 Qdrant Hot-Path And Contention Audit
verbatim: Candidate payload bytes fall without losing ranking or citation fields.
action: Close via L758's differential test plus byte accounting: candidate payload bytes per query drop (target ~50% per L748) while ranked output, citations, and diagnostics fields are byte-identical to the full-payload control.
where: hot candidate search path; trace result-bytes field (from L756)
why: This is the acceptance that L758 bought bytes without silently losing ranking/citation inputs.
feasibility_note: Pure consequence of L758 + L756 instrumentation; no independent risk. The 'without losing fields' half is the same differential test demanded there — one artifact satisfies both lines. Numeric target is pre-set by the L748 measurement (292->148 KB band), matching the hypothesis-promotion discipline.
required_receipt: Per-query bytes before/after (from trace) + identical ranked/cited output diff; both attached to the L758 receipt.

section: P1.9 Qdrant Hot-Path And Contention Audit
verbatim: Query latency during a bounded ingest stays within the measured target and ingestion remains durable/queryable.
action: During a bounded ingest (canary or S4 tranche), run the three-tier latency probe concurrently and assert query latency stays within the L746-derived target while the ingest completes durably and its points are searchable afterward.
who: script (concurrent probe: probe_tier_latency.py + bounded ingest driver)
where: deployed backend + ingest-worker container + Qdrant; UGO_CORPUS canary first
why: The S4 paid pass is exactly this scenario at scale; this acceptance is its preflight canary.
feasibility_note: Well-posed only after 'measured target' is pinned: L746 gives Qdrant-side ms targets but end-to-end query latency needs the agreed p95 from L796's 'agreed p95' — set the number BEFORE the run (preflight-canary discipline from memory). Depends on L768's profiles if the default config fails the probe. Durability half is cheap: post-ingest count + sample search of newly-written points.
required_receipt: Concurrent-run report: query p50/p95 during ingest vs target, ingest completion (counts match), and post-ingest searchability of new points; exit 0.

section: P1.9 Qdrant Hot-Path And Contention Audit
verbatim: End-to-end latency reporting distinguishes Qdrant time from embedding, planning, vocabulary, reranking, Mongo hydration, Neo4j, and synthesis.
action: Extend the retrieval trace/latency report to attribute end-to-end time across: Qdrant, embedding, planning, vocabulary, reranking, Mongo hydration, Neo4j, synthesis — and assert the parts sum to within a tolerance of the whole on live queries.
who: junior-agent (trace schema + report change, building on L756)
where: backend trace/diagnostics schema; probe_tier_latency.py report; chat trace metadata
why: Stage attribution is what turns 'Fast is 39.8s degraded' from a mystery into a work item.
feasibility_note: Superset of L756 (which covers the Qdrant stages); most non-Qdrant stages already have partial timing in traces — the work is normalizing to one report with a sum-check so unattributed time is visible instead of hidden. Must land before S8's A/B for the same reason as L756. The sum-vs-total tolerance check is the anti-silent-gap device (silent-fallback accounting rule).
required_receipt: One live query report listing all eight stage timings + unattributed remainder < tolerance; three-tier probe output includes the breakdown; exit 0.

section: P1.9 Qdrant Hot-Path And Contention Audit
verbatim: Verify ingestion/backfill calls consistently use lower-priority classes.
action: Verify by grep + live trace that every ingestion/backfill embed and rerank call declares a lower-priority (backfill/bulk) class: enumerate caller sites in ingest-worker and backfill scripts, then run one backfill tranche and read the sidecar's admission log/stats for class labels.
where: ingest-worker container call sites; backfill scripts (e.g. summary_backfill.py); embedder_mlx :8082 admission stats
why: One mislabeled bulk caller at interactive class defeats the whole P1.8 isolation design during the paid pass.
feasibility_note: Preflight for S4 (preflight-canary discipline). The S0 warmup receipt incidentally proves the backfill class is honored server-side ('warmup ... at backfill class'); what remains is caller coverage — including the OFFLINE ingest-worker container's code path, which is a different container from the backend (memory: offline-ingest overlay) and is easy to miss in a backend-only grep.
required_receipt: Caller audit table (site x declared class) with zero interactive-class bulk callers + one tranche's admission stats showing all bulk requests at backfill class; restamp box.

section: P1.9 Qdrant Hot-Path And Contention Audit
verbatim: Add or verify reranker-side priority admission.
action: Audit the deployed :8081 reranker for admission/priority classes equivalent to the embedder's; if absent, port the embedder_mlx admission-gate pattern (priority/FIFO classes + queue caps) to the reranker sidecar and verify with a two-class contention probe.
where: :8081 torch fp16 reranker sidecar (plist-managed, ~/PolymathRuntime copy); rollback reranker_mlx
why: Backfill-driven rerank load can starve interactive reranking exactly as embedding load did before P1.8.
feasibility_note: 'Add or verify' — verification first: no receipt exists either way. Registry warning applies (registry_leftover 'reranker readiness'): the deployed binary is the torch fp16 cross-encoder, reranker_mlx is rollback — audit the binary actually serving :8081, and remember sidecars deploy from the ~/PolymathRuntime COPY (repo edits need re-install). Contention design note from memory: rerank and embed share one Metal GPU, and support-pass reranking is already gated off (RERANK_EVIDENCE_SUPPORT) — admission classes complement, not replace, that rule. Also mind the reranker death-spiral rule: any queueing added must never split-retry timed-out requests.
required_receipt: Either an audit receipt showing existing class support on :8081 (config + stats) or the ported gate with a probe: interactive rerank admitted ahead of a queued bulk batch; suite exit 0.

section: P1.9 Qdrant Hot-Path And Contention Audit
verbatim: Measure whether keepalive provides value after startup warmup.
action: Measure keepalive value post-warmup: after S0's warmup (L732), compare first-request latency after 10/30/60-minute idle windows with and without a periodic keepalive ping on embedder (:8082) and reranker (:8081); adopt keepalive only if idle decay is demonstrated.
where: embedder_mlx :8082, reranker :8081; optional keepalive timer (launchd or backend task)
why: Warmup fixed cold start; keepalive is only worth its GPU pings if MPS/Metal actually decays warm state during idle.
feasibility_note: Cheap, honest experiment with a built-in null hypothesis — the correct default is NO keepalive unless decay is measured (avoid adding standing GPU load to a contended Metal device per the GPU-contention rule). Registry disambiguation: 'keepalive after startup warmup' is a distinct concern from P1.8 inference warmup and from Qdrant compile cost (registry_leftover 'inference warmup' note) — do not restamp P1.8 boxes off this measurement.
required_receipt: Idle-decay table: first-call latency vs idle duration, keepalive on/off, both sidecars; decision line (adopt/skip) with numbers; if adopted, the keepalive mechanism + its measured cost.

section: P1.9 Qdrant Hot-Path And Contention Audit
verbatim: Evaluate separate ingestion embedder capacity only if contention persists. (merged + deployed both accounts + live-verified 2026-07-13 ...)
action: Accept receipt (verified 2026-07-13): separate ingestion embed capacity EXISTS and is validated — RunPod embed lane merged + deployed on both accounts (k695blmk52oscm / hlp9h3o4zd0v4d), dim 1024, dual-account routing live-verified. Remaining closure: record whether local contention persists post-P1.8 (the item's own trigger condition) and keep use inside the L790 promotion gate.
who: junior-agent (receipt acceptance + post-P1.8 contention measurement); owner (any expansion of use)
where: runpod_flash_embedder/ lane; RunPod endpoints on both accounts; local embedder_mlx :8082 contention stats
why: Off-box embed capacity is the escape valve if backfill still contends with interactive embedding after admission classes.
feasibility_note: The evaluation the box asks for is done and receipted in the ledger itself (live-verified 2026-07-13). The conditional ('only if contention persists') is now the open half: post-S0, contention should be re-measured under a bulk pass (rides L786/L796 probes at S4) before treating RunPod embed as the default bulk lane. Registry: 'RunPod' is two lanes (extraction vs embed) with different contracts (registry_extraction 'RunPod' OVERLAPPING) — this receipt covers the EMBED lane only.
required_receipt: Ledger receipt line (both account IDs, dim 1024, dual routing) + an S4-window contention reading that either justifies bulk use of the RunPod lane or shows local classes suffice.

section: P1.9 Qdrant Hot-Path And Contention Audit
verbatim: live-verified 2026-07-13: dim 1024, dual-account routing works; PROMOTION GATE: local-mxfp8 vs remote-fp cosine = 0.98, so runpod embed mode is approved for whole-corpus/bulk experiments only until a held-out recall A/B clears mixed-source use — never mix embedding sources within one collection
action: Enforce the promotion gate as standing policy: RunPod embed (remote-fp) approved for whole-corpus/bulk experiments ONLY; never mix embedding sources within one collection; mixed-source use stays blocked until a held-out recall A/B clears it. Re-check the 0.98 cosine receipt if either side's model/quantization changes.
who: owner (gate release decision after A/B); script (cosine parity re-probe on model change)
where: runpod_flash_embedder lane vs local embedder_mlx (Qwen3-Embedding-0.6B-mxfp8); every Qdrant collection's embedding-source provenance
why: 0.98 cosine between local-mxfp8 and remote-fp is close but NOT identity — mixing sources in one collection would skew neighbor geometry unpredictably.
feasibility_note: Sound, receipted gate (2026-07-13, cosine 0.98, dim 1024). The never-mix rule needs a mechanical guard to be real: collections currently carry no enforced embedding-source field — consider stamping embed_source in collection metadata/payload at the next S5/S7 rewrite so the invariant is checkable, else it lives only in prose (Standing Rule 2 spirit). The held-out recall A/B that could relax the gate naturally lands with the S8/S10 eval harness; until then whole-corpus experiments must be all-remote or all-local per collection.
required_receipt: Standing: per-collection single embed source (all-local or all-remote) verifiable by census; gate release requires a held-out recall A/B report (local vs remote vs mixed) with no recall loss, owner-signed.

section: P1.9 Qdrant Hot-Path And Contention Audit
verbatim: The first interactive embedding after service startup does not pay model compile/inference warmup cost.
action: Close by probe: restart embedder (:8082) and reranker (:8081) sidecars, wait for inference_ready=true, then time the FIRST interactive embed and rerank calls; assert they fall within ~1.5x steady-state (no compile spike), banking the S0 warmup work as user-visible.
where: embedder_mlx :8082, reranker :8081; backend query path
why: This acceptance is the entire point of the P1.8 warmup train: the first user query after a deploy pays nothing extra.
feasibility_note: All enabling work is merged and receipted (L732/L735/L738 at 327da45; L729 [x] for the reranker) — this box is one probe away and SHOULD have ridden S0's receipt train; it is stale-open in the same sense Standing Rule 3 targets. Note the warmup receipt (duration 0.045s) measures warmup itself, not the first POST-warmup interactive call, which is what this box asserts — the probe must measure the latter through the admission gate at interactive class.
required_receipt: Post-restart timing: warmup completes, then first interactive embed and first rerank within 1.5x steady-state p50; probe exit 0; box stamped with date.

section: P1.9 Qdrant Hot-Path And Contention Audit
verbatim: Backfill cannot force interactive embedding/reranking beyond agreed p95.
action: Set the agreed interactive p95 number in advance (from the L746/L744 baseline + current three-tier probe), then run a sustained backfill while firing interactive queries; assert interactive embed+rerank p95 stays under the agreed bound thanks to admission classes.
who: script (contention probe harness); owner sets/accepts the p95 number
where: embedder_mlx :8082 + reranker :8081 admission gates; backfill driver (ingest-worker); three-tier probe
why: This is the load-bearing guarantee of the isolation program: paid backfills cannot degrade the user's chat.
feasibility_note: Mechanism is in place (L730 classes, L786 caller audit pending); what is missing is the 'agreed p95' — no numeric target exists yet in the ledger, and per the hypothesis-promotion rule it must be fixed BEFORE the probe run, not read off the result. Natural slot: preflight + during the S4 paid pass, sharing harness with L782/L786. Reranker-side coverage depends on L787 landing first (embedder-only classes leave a rerank hole in this guarantee).
required_receipt: Named p95 target recorded first; then contention-run report showing interactive p95 <= target while backfill sustains; both attached to the S4 preflight.

section: P1.9 Qdrant Hot-Path And Contention Audit
verbatim: Cold-start and concurrent-query tests no longer degrade unexpectedly.
action: Add cold-start and concurrent-query scenarios to the standing latency harness (restart-then-query; N parallel queries) and run them before/after each P1 change; assert no unexplained degradation vs the recorded baseline band.
where: deployed backend + sidecars; docs/baselines latency artifacts
why: Regression detection is what makes every earlier P1 receipt durable instead of a one-day snapshot.
feasibility_note: 'No longer degrade unexpectedly' is only falsifiable against a recorded expectation — the harness must persist a baseline band (p50/p95 + tolerance) per scenario. Cold-start scenario is cheap post-L794; concurrent-query needs care on the shared Metal GPU (parallel rerank is a known pathology per the GPU-contention rule — the harness should measure, not mask, that). Slot at S8 where the A/B machinery runs anyway; earlier ad-hoc runs welcome.
required_receipt: Harness output: cold-start and concurrent scenarios with recorded baseline bands and a green run post-change; wired into the S8 A/B and S14 regression matrix.

section: P1.9 Make Reranking Adaptive
verbatim: Measure reranker latency by candidate count and token length.
action: Instrument and sweep: measure deployed :8081 rerank latency across a grid of candidate counts (e.g. 10/25/50/85) x passage token lengths, producing a cost model table that L803's budget selection will consume.
where: :8081 torch fp16 cross-encoder sidecar; probe artifact in docs/baselines/
why: Adaptive budgets need a real cost curve; without it 'adaptive' is guessing.
feasibility_note: Pure measurement, no risk. Section-id note: this 'Make Reranking Adaptive' section is the SECOND section labeled P1.9 (critique C4 flags the duplicate id) — receipts should cite 'P1.9-adaptive' to stay unambiguous. Run the sweep serially (never parallel rerank batches on the shared Metal GPU per the contention rule) and on the true fp16 cross-encoder now serving :8081, not the rollback MLX backend.
required_receipt: Latency grid artifact (candidates x token-length -> p50/p95) with sidecar backend identity recorded; filed under docs/baselines with date.

section: P1.9 Make Reranking Adaptive
verbatim: Generate query-relevant excerpts before cross-encoder scoring.
action: Prototype query-relevant excerpting (window selection around match spans) feeding the cross-encoder shorter passages; gate adoption on a held-out A/B showing latency gain with zero recall/nDCG loss vs full-passage scoring.
where: backend rerank preparation path (candidate text assembly before :8081 call); held-out eval set
why: CE cost scales with token length; excerpts attack the second axis of L801's cost model.
feasibility_note: Changes the CE's INPUT — under the production-redesign direction the cross-encoder is the sole scoring authority, so feeding it excerpts can silently change ranking semantics; evidence relevant outside the excerpt window is scored as absent. This is exactly the class needing the P1.1 held-out gate (hypothesis-promotion rule: numeric recall/nDCG targets fixed in advance). Do not ship as a default without the A/B; consider shape-conditional use (long list/synthesis passages) per L805.
required_receipt: Held-out A/B report: rerank latency delta + recall/nDCG deltas (target: latency down, quality delta >= 0) vs full-passage control; owner-visible adopt/skip decision line.

section: P1.9 Make Reranking Adaptive
verbatim: Select candidate budget by answer shape, score cliff, and retrieval uncertainty.
action: Implement candidate-budget selection using (a) answer shape (P1.6 shape router), (b) score-cliff detection on retrieval scores, (c) retrieval-uncertainty signal; consume L801's cost table to pick the budget; verify with per-shape before/after latency and held-out quality.
where: backend rerank dispatch (candidate count selection before :8081); consumes P1.6 answer-shape routing + L801 cost model
why: Most queries need far fewer than max candidates reranked; cliffs and shape tell us which.
feasibility_note: Correctly sequenced only at/after S9: 'answer shape' has no implementation yet (P1.6 all boxes open, PLAN_CRITIQUE sec.1) — building shape-conditional budgets before shape routing exists would hardcode a stub. Score-cliff machinery has precedent in P1.6's 'dynamic breadth/K based on score cliffs' — implement ONCE and share, or the two items become duplicate cliff detectors (registry failure-mode (b) pattern). Held-out gate mandatory as with L802.
required_receipt: Policy code + per-shape budget table; held-out A/B: latency down materially, recall/nDCG flat; trace shows chosen budget + trigger (shape/cliff/uncertainty) per query.

section: P1.9 Make Reranking Adaptive
verbatim: Preserve broader pools for cross-domain and list questions.
action: Encode a floor rule in the L803 policy: cross-domain and list/enumeration shapes keep expanded candidate pools (no aggressive budget cuts); add held-out cases of both shapes to the A/B so the floor is asserted, not assumed.
where: rerank budget policy (with L803); held-out eval set list/cross-domain rows
why: Budget cuts hurt exactly the query shapes that need breadth — this floor protects them by construction.
feasibility_note: A constraint on L803 rather than separate work — same change, same A/B. It also aligns with the three-tier regression matrix rows (list, cross-domain, cross-corpus are named row shapes) so the S14 matrix will re-assert it. Shape detection dependency is the same P1.6 prerequisite as L803.
required_receipt: Policy test: list + cross-domain queries receive >= floor candidate budgets; held-out list/cross-domain rows show no recall loss under the adaptive policy.

section: P1.9 Make Reranking Adaptive
verbatim: Evaluate cascade or late-interaction alternatives before model changes.
action: Before any reranker MODEL change (P3.5 territory), run a bounded evaluation of (a) cascade reranking (cheap scorer prunes, CE scores survivors) and (b) late-interaction (ColBERT-style) alternatives on the held-out set, comparing latency/quality against the adaptive-budget CE from L803.
who: junior-agent (experiment harness); owner (any model adoption decision)
where: experiment branch; :8081 CE as control; held-out eval set
why: Cheaper architectures may beat budget-tuning, but only measurement — not fashion — should replace the CE.
feasibility_note: Explicitly an ordering rule ('before model changes') — it gates P3.5 (reranker serving experiment, S13) and inherits Experiment status: no production adoption without before/after evaluation. Under the production-redesign decision the CE is the sole scoring authority, so any cascade's cheap first stage must be shown not to prune CE-winners (measure first-stage recall@survivors). Do this only after L801-L805 land, since the adaptive CE is the honest baseline to beat.
required_receipt: Experiment report: cascade and late-interaction vs adaptive-CE control on held-out (latency, recall/nDCG, first-stage prune loss); explicit adopt/reject line signed by owner.

section: P1.9 Make Reranking Adaptive
verbatim: Rerank latency improves materially with no held-out recall/nDCG loss.
action: Acceptance A/B for the adaptive-rerank train (L801-L805): rerank stage latency improves materially (set the numeric bar from the L801 cost table in advance) with held-out recall/nDCG deltas >= 0 across shapes.
where: rerank stage traces + held-out eval artifacts (EVAL_*.json)
why: The single gate that converts the adaptive-rerank hypothesis into a promoted behavior.
feasibility_note: Depends on P1.1 held-out set being complete with the contamination check (P1.1 has 3 open boxes incl. the automated contamination eval-run check, which the plan lands at S10 — run at S9 with at least the manual contamination discipline). 'Materially' must be pre-quantified (hypothesis-promotion rule) — propose: p50 rerank stage time down >= 30% on non-list shapes, from the L801 curve.
required_receipt: A/B report: rerank p50/p95 before/after vs the pre-registered bar + recall/nDCG per shape (no loss); attached to the ledger line with date.

section: P1.9 Make Reranking Adaptive
verbatim: Fast is meaningfully faster than Hybrid while remaining useful.
action: Measure tier separation post-changes: run the three-tier latency probe and assert qdrant_only (Fast) p50 is materially below qdrant_mongo (Hybrid) while Fast's held-out answer quality stays above its usefulness floor.
where: three retrieval tiers of the deployed backend; probe + eval artifacts
why: If Fast is not actually fast, the tier contract is marketing — users pay Hybrid cost either way.
feasibility_note: Terminology (registry_leftover/registry_retrieval): 'Fast' = RetrievalTier.qdrant_only, 'Hybrid' = RetrievalTier.qdrant_mongo — and 'hybrid' ALSO names dense+sparse vector search inside Qdrant, an orthogonal mechanism; keep receipts in tier enum names. Known bad datum: ledger header records 'Fast 39.8s degraded' — this box is currently FAILING on its face, and the fix is the whole P1 train (cache L706, warmup L732, batching L703, adaptive rerank) rather than a single item; assert it after S9. 'Remaining useful' needs a defined floor (held-out Fast-tier pass rate) set in advance.
required_receipt: Three-tier probe: qdrant_only p50/p95 materially below qdrant_mongo (pre-registered ratio) + Fast-tier held-out pass rate >= floor; filed with tier enum names and date.
